title: Lamb Weston (LW) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Lamb Weston (LW). Economic Prospect Score: 55/100 — Moderate Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A modest 5% growth rate assumes steady global volume increases for french fries, offset by the challenges of a softer consumer environment and fluctuating input costs.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8% discount rate reflects the highly defensive, staple nature of the food processing sector, balancing the lower risk profile with agricultural commodity volatility.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2% terminal rate aligns perfectly with long-term inflation and global population growth, which is the ultimate driver for basic food staples.

thesisParagraphs: Lamb Weston operates as a vital node in the global food supply chain, converting raw agricultural potatoes into the ubiquitous frozen french fries demanded by restaurants worldwide. Operating in a consolidated oligopoly, the company benefits from the immense scale required to service global quick-service giants, creating a barrier to entry for smaller competitors.

While the fundamental demand for the product is highly resilient, the financial profile is that of a heavy industrial processor. The company generates roughly $230M in free cash flow, but must constantly reinvest massive amounts of capital into large-scale facilities to chase global volume growth. The valuation reflects this reality: a reliable, slow-growing staple that demands conservative cash flow assumptions.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why is the growth rate only 5%?
faq.answer: The core product is a mature, commoditized food staple. Growth is inherently limited to population expansion and incremental increases in global dining-out frequency.

faq.question: What is the biggest operational risk?
faq.answer: Poor potato crop yields due to adverse weather or disease can severely impact margins, as raw potatoes are the primary and indispensable input cost.

faq.question: Why is it capital intensive?
faq.answer: Processing millions of tons of potatoes requires massive, specialized industrial facilities and complex cold-storage supply chains that require constant investment.

prospectVerdictDetail: Lamb Weston operates as a dominant force in the global frozen potato processing market. The business benefits from a defensive consumer staples profile and consistent long-term demand for french fries globally. However, the company faces near-term margin pressures from fluctuating commodity costs, challenging restaurant traffic, and the sheer capital intensity required to expand global production capacity.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Assessment of the company's competitive momentum, market share trajectory, and product velocity.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs Peers
factors.rationale: Revenue growth is steady but unexceptional, typical for a mature food processor, driven by long-term volume increases and pricing actions.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: Maintains a leading global market share in frozen potato products, acting as a crucial supplier to major quick-service restaurant chains worldwide.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Pricing power is limited. While the company can pass on significant commodity inflation, doing so risks volume declines if restaurant partners push back or consumers trade down.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Innovation is slow-paced in this mature category, focusing mostly on operational efficiency and slight variations in cut or seasoning rather than disruptive new products.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Evaluation of the durability of the company's economic moat, including switching costs, network effects, and capital intensity.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Moderate switching costs exist due to the massive scale and reliability required by global restaurant chains, making it difficult for them to easily replace a primary supplier.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: The business exhibits minimal network effects. The value of the product does not inherently increase with the number of consumers eating it.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Operates under standard agricultural and food safety regulations. IP is primarily process-oriented rather than heavily defensible consumer patents.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: The business model is highly capital-intensive, requiring massive ongoing investments in vast processing facilities, storage infrastructure, and supply chain logistics.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Analysis of market sentiment, earnings estimate revisions, and management capital allocation.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Estimates have been volatile, reflecting the push and pull of input cost inflation against the company's ability to execute pricing actions.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: Sentiment remains neutral. It is viewed as a reliable defensive staple, but lacks the growth narrative to command a premium multiple.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management has navigated difficult agricultural cycles reasonably well, prioritizing essential capacity expansion to meet long-term global demand.

prospectKeyRisks: Fluctuations in potato crop yields due to adverse weather or disease.
Weakening consumer traffic at major quick-service restaurant chains.
Intense price competition in the commoditized segments of the frozen foods market.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Successful execution of capacity expansion projects in high-growth international markets.
A faster-than-expected recovery in global restaurant and foodservice traffic.
Implementation of cost-saving initiatives and supply chain optimization to improve margins.
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored LW at 55/100 and Opus at 56/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
